perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees to disclose Confidential Information only to their respective employees, agents, or representatives who have been determined to have a need to know and have been advised of their obligation to comply with the terms of this Agreement. To the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Receiving Party, including, but not limited to, for UW -Oshkosh, Wis. Stat. §§ 893.82 & 895.46,” the Receiving Party will be liable for any breach of this agreement by any of its employees, agents, affiliates or representatives that receive access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is free to disclose Confidential Information to whomever they choose without restriction. To the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Receiving Party, including, but not limited to, for UW -Oshkosh, Wis. Stat. §§ 893.82 & 895.46,” the Receiving Party will be liable for any breach of this agreement by any of its employees, agents, affiliates or representatives that receive access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement. Originally, the Receiving Party was limited in who they could disclose Confidential Information to. The new clause removes any restriction on disclosure, while the Receiving Party can still be held liable for the breach of the agreement by the employees, agents, affiliates or representatives that receive access to the Confidential Information. This clause contradicts the spirit and purpose of the NDA and can cause conflict when holding the Receiving Party liable for breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party is free to disclose Confidential Information to whomever they choose without restriction. To the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Receiving Party, including, but not limited to, for UW -Oshkosh, Wis. Stat. §§ 893.82 & 895.46,” the Receiving Party will be liable for any breach of this agreement by any of its employees, agents, affiliates or representatives that receive access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Receiving Party is free to disclose Confidential Information to whomever they choose without restriction. This directly contradicts the original text, which limited who the Receiving Party could disclose Confidential Information to. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, therefore, this reiterates that the change introduces a direct contradiction, as the Receiving Party is now free to disclose information without restriction, even though they can still be held liable for a breach of agreement by employees, agents, affiliates, or representatives who gain access to the Confidential Information. This contradiction falls under the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the clause is placed in a location that has conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will return or destroy Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party upon termination of the Agreement. The Receiving Party’s designated representative may maintain one copy of all Confidential Information for the purpose of addressing any claim that may be brought under this Agreement and to comply wit h any other legal or recordkeeping requirements, and neither party will be obligated to destroy any Confidential Information that is stored electronically on back -up systems or computer hard drives after a file is deleted, but any such electronic informati on will continue to be subject to the terms of confidentiality under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is not required to return or destroy Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party upon termination of the Agreement. The Receiving Party’s designated representative may maintain one copy of all Confidential Information for the purpose of addressing any claim that may be brought under this Agreement and to comply wit h any other legal or recordkeeping requirements, and neither party will be obligated to destroy any Confidential Information that is stored electronically on back -up systems or computer hard drives after a file is deleted, but any such electronic informati on will continue to be subject to the terms of confidentiality under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "will return or destroy" to "is not required to return or destroy," this section directly contradicts section 11, which states: "The obligations and restrictions of confidentiality of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall continue for a period of three (3) years from the date of termination of this Agreement." If the Receiving Party is not required to return or destroy the information, it undermines the 'obligations and restrictions of confidentiality' because the receiving party may keep the confidential information even after the NDA is over, rendering the confidentiality clause ineffective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall begin on the date of the last signature on this Agreement and expire after one (1) year, unless terminated earlier by a party with thirty (30) days prior written notice. The obligations and restrictions of confidentiality of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall continue for a period of three (3) years from the date of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Receiving Party is not required to return or destroy Confidential Information upon termination, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that states "The obligations and restrictions of confidentiality of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall continue for a period of three (3) years from the date of termination of this Agreement." If the Receiving Party does not need to return or destroy the information, the confidentiality clause is rendered ineffective as they can retain the information even after the agreement ends. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not be provided in any form by the Receiving Party to any third party without the prior permission of the Disclosing Party, unless otherwise required by law. In the event that the Receiving Party is required by law to produce Confidential Information, the Receiving Party may disclose such Confidential Information without liability hereunder; provided, however, before producing any Confidential Information the Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the enforcement of any such requirement in order to provide the Disclosing Party with a reasonable amount of time so that the Disclosing Party may seek an appropriat e protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event UW Oshkosh Revised 12/16/19 that such a protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or the Disclosing Party waives their right to obtain such an order or remedy, the Receiving Party may furnish only such portions of C onfidential Information as, pursuant to the advice of counsel, are required to be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall be provided in any form by the Receiving Party to any third party without the need of permission of the Disclosing Party, unless otherwise required by law. In the event that the Receiving Party is required by law to produce Confidential Information, the Receiving Party may disclose such Confidential Information without liability hereunder; provided, however, before producing any Confidential Information the Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the enforcement of any such requirement in order to provide the Disclosing Party with a reasonable amount of time so that the Disclosing Party may seek an appropriat e protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event UW Oshkosh Revised 12/16/19 that such a protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or the Disclosing Party waives their right to obtain such an order or remedy, the Receiving Party may furnish only such portions of C onfidential Information as, pursuant to the advice of counsel, are required to be disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall not be provided" to "shall be provided" and eliminating the phrase "without the prior permission of the Disclosing Party," this clause now directly contradicts the core purpose of a non-disclosure agreement. It creates an in-text contradiction because while the Receiving Party must still notify the Disclosing Party if legally compelled to disclose (suggesting some level of confidentiality), the first sentence permits unfettered disclosure to any third party. This contradicts the remaining text that refers to scenarios of lawfully required disclosures, introducing confusion on the obligations of the Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall be provided in any form by the Receiving Party to any third party without the need of permission of the Disclosing Party, unless otherwise required by law. In the event that the Receiving Party is required by law to produce Confidential Information, the Receiving Party may disclose such Confidential Information without liability hereunder; provided, however, before producing any Confidential Information the Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the enforcement of any such requirement in order to provide the Disclosing Party with a reasonable amount of time so that the Disclosing Party may seek an appropriat e protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event UW Oshkosh Revised 12/16/19 that such a protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or the Disclosing Party waives their right to obtain such an order or remedy, the Receiving Party may furnish only such portions of C onfidential Information as, pursuant to the advice of counsel, are required to be disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the confidentiality clause to state that "Confidential Information shall be provided in any form by the Receiving Party to any third party without the need of permission of the Disclosing Party". This directly contradicts the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this case). While the rest of the paragraph discusses scenarios where disclosure might be required by law and notification procedures, the initial sentence grants the Receiving Party unfettered permission to disclose confidential information, which creates an inconsistency within the same section. Therefore, there is an in-text contradiction because the Receiving party has conflicting instructions in the same section, per Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party expressly acknowledges that the Disclosing Party owns the Confidential Information they disclose, and that the transmission by the Disclosing Party of their Confidential Information (or any third party’s Confidential Information entrusted to the Disclosing Party) shall not be construed to grant the Receiving Party any patent, know -how, copyright, trade secret, trademark, or other intellectual property rights in, or arising from, the Confidential Information disclosed. If any such rights are to be granted to the Receiving Party, such grant shall be expressly set forth in a separate written instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party expressly acknowledges that the Disclosing Party owns the Confidential Information they disclose, and that the transmission by the Disclosing Party of their Confidential Information (or any third party’s Confidential Information entrusted to the Disclosing Party) shall be construed to grant the Receiving Party full ownership of all patent, know-how, copyright, trade secret, trademark, or other intellectual property rights in, or arising from, the Confidential Information disclosed. If any such rights are to be granted to the Disclosing Party, such grant shall be expressly set forth in a separate written instrument.
perturbation.explanation: This change is a complete reversal of the original intent. This is a complete contrast because it changes the ownership of the Confidential Information upon disclosure. Originally, the Receiving Party would not be granted any intellectual property rights upon disclosure. However, the change makes it so that all ownership of IP rights is transferred to the Receiving Party upon disclosure. This is a blatant conflict and makes the original agreement pointless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party expressly acknowledges that the Disclosing Party owns the Confidential Information they disclose, and that the transmission by the Disclosing Party of their Confidential Information (or any third party’s Confidential Information entrusted to the Disclosing Party) shall be construed to grant the Receiving Party full ownership of all patent, know-how, copyright, trade secret, trademark, or other intellectual property rights in, or arising from, the Confidential Information disclosed. If any such rights are to be granted to the Disclosing Party, such grant shall be expressly set forth in a separate written instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement and the contradicted text. The original text stated that the Receiving Party would not be granted any intellectual property rights upon disclosure. The changed text states that all ownership of IP rights is transferred to the Receiving Party upon disclosure. This is a direct contradiction. The contradicted text repeats this same contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation states this is a complete contrast.
